findings.name: Hardcoded AWS access key ID in Terraform config
findings.description: Hardcoded AWS access key IDs committed to version control expose the credentials to anyone with repository access.
findings.recommendation: Remove hardcoded credentials. Use IAM roles, environment variables, or AWS Secrets Manager.

findings.name: Subnet configured to assign public IP addresses automatically
findings.description: Subnets that auto-assign public IPs expose every launched instance to the internet by default.
findings.recommendation: Set map_public_ip_on_launch = false. Explicitly assign Elastic IPs only where required.

findings.name: Subnet configured to assign public IP addresses automatically
findings.description: Subnets that auto-assign public IPs expose every launched instance to the internet by default.
findings.recommendation: Set map_public_ip_on_launch = false. Explicitly assign Elastic IPs only where required.

findings.name: Security Group allows SSH (22) from any IP
findings.description: A security group ingress rule permits SSH access from the entire internet (0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0), enabling brute-force and exploitation attacks.
findings.recommendation: Restrict SSH access to specific trusted IP ranges or use AWS Systems Manager Session Manager to eliminate the need for SSH.

findings.name: Security Group allows SSH (22) from any IP
findings.description: A security group ingress rule permits SSH access from the entire internet (0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0), enabling brute-force and exploitation attacks.
findings.recommendation: Restrict SSH access to specific trusted IP ranges or use AWS Systems Manager Session Manager to eliminate the need for SSH.

findings.name: Security Group allows SSH (22) from any IP
findings.description: A security group ingress rule permits SSH access from the entire internet (0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0), enabling brute-force and exploitation attacks.
findings.recommendation: Restrict SSH access to specific trusted IP ranges or use AWS Systems Manager Session Manager to eliminate the need for SSH.

findings.name: Security Group allows SSH (22) from any IP
findings.description: A security group ingress rule permits SSH access from the entire internet (0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0), enabling brute-force and exploitation attacks.
findings.recommendation: Restrict SSH access to specific trusted IP ranges or use AWS Systems Manager Session Manager to eliminate the need for SSH.

findings.name: S3 bucket ACL allows public read
findings.description: Setting acl=public-read allows any internet user to list and download all bucket objects.
findings.recommendation: Remove the public ACL. Use pre-signed URLs or CloudFront OAC for public content delivery.

findings.name: S3 Block Public ACLs disabled
findings.description: Disabling block_public_acls allows public ACLs to be applied, potentially exposing objects.
findings.recommendation: Set block_public_acls = true in aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block.

findings.name: S3 Block Public Policy disabled
findings.description: Disabling block_public_policy allows bucket policies granting public access to be applied.
findings.recommendation: Set block_public_policy = true in aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block.

findings.name: S3 Ignore Public ACLs disabled
findings.description: Setting ignore_public_acls=false means existing public ACLs on objects remain active.
findings.recommendation: Set ignore_public_acls = true to ignore any public ACLs on the bucket and objects.

findings.name: S3 Restrict Public Buckets disabled
findings.description: Disabling restrict_public_buckets allows cross-account policies granting public access.
findings.recommendation: Set restrict_public_buckets = true to block public and cross-account access policies.

findings.name: EC2 IMDSv1 enabled — instance metadata service token not required
findings.description: IMDSv1 allows any code running on the instance to access instance metadata and IAM credentials without authentication, enabling SSRF-to-credential-theft attacks.
findings.recommendation: Set http_tokens = 'required' in metadata_options to enforce IMDSv2 and prevent SSRF attacks.

findings.name: EC2 instance assigned public IP address
findings.description: Directly assigning a public IP increases attack surface. Instances should use NAT gateways or load balancers for outbound/inbound traffic.
findings.recommendation: Use private subnets for EC2 instances. Route traffic through NAT gateways and application load balancers.

findings.name: EBS volume not encrypted
findings.description: Unencrypted EBS volumes expose data if the underlying storage is compromised or the snapshot is shared.
findings.recommendation: Set encrypted = true on all EBS volumes. Enable account-level EBS encryption by default in AWS settings.

findings.name: EBS volume not encrypted
findings.description: Unencrypted EBS volumes expose data if the underlying storage is compromised or the snapshot is shared.
findings.recommendation: Set encrypted = true on all EBS volumes. Enable account-level EBS encryption by default in AWS settings.

findings.name: Hardcoded password or secret in Terraform resource
findings.description: Hardcoded passwords or secrets in Terraform files are exposed to anyone with access to the code repository.
findings.recommendation: Use AWS Secrets Manager or SSM Parameter Store with data sources to inject secrets at runtime.

findings.name: RDS instance publicly accessible
findings.description: Making the RDS instance publicly accessible exposes the database endpoint to the internet, enabling brute-force and injection attacks.
findings.recommendation: Set publicly_accessible = false. Place RDS instances in private subnets accessible only from application tier.

findings.name: EBS volume not encrypted
findings.description: Unencrypted EBS volumes expose data if the underlying storage is compromised or the snapshot is shared.
findings.recommendation: Set encrypted = true on all EBS volumes. Enable account-level EBS encryption by default in AWS settings.

findings.name: RDS storage not encrypted
findings.description: Unencrypted RDS storage exposes database data if the underlying storage is compromised or snapshots are shared.
findings.recommendation: Set storage_encrypted = true and specify a KMS key with kms_key_id.

findings.name: RDS automated backup retention disabled (0 days)
findings.description: Setting backup_retention_period=0 disables automated backups, preventing point-in-time recovery.
findings.recommendation: Set backup_retention_period to at least 7 days (35 for compliance-sensitive workloads).

findings.name: RDS deletion protection disabled
findings.description: Without deletion protection, the database can be accidentally or maliciously deleted, causing data loss.
findings.recommendation: Set deletion_protection = true for all production RDS instances.

findings.name: RDS Multi-AZ not enabled — single point of failure
findings.description: Single-AZ RDS instances have no automatic failover, causing downtime during maintenance or AZ failures.
findings.recommendation: Set multi_az = true for production databases to enable automatic failover.

findings.name: RDS final snapshot skipped — data loss risk on deletion
findings.description: Setting skip_final_snapshot=true means no backup is taken before the database is destroyed, risking permanent data loss.
findings.recommendation: Set skip_final_snapshot = false and specify a final_snapshot_identifier.

findings.name: CloudTrail log file validation disabled
findings.description: Without log file validation, CloudTrail logs can be silently tampered with, undermining audit integrity.
findings.recommendation: Set enable_log_file_validation = true to enable SHA-256 hash validation of log files.

findings.name: CloudTrail not configured as multi-region trail
findings.description: A single-region CloudTrail misses API activity in other regions, creating blind spots for attackers.
findings.recommendation: Set is_multi_region_trail = true to capture API activity in all AWS regions.

findings.name: CloudTrail global service events not included
findings.description: Excluding global services (IAM, STS, CloudFront) from CloudTrail creates blind spots for IAM activity.
findings.recommendation: Set include_global_service_events = true to capture IAM, STS, and CloudFront API calls.

findings.name: KMS key automatic rotation disabled
findings.description: Without automatic rotation, long-lived KMS keys increase the blast radius of key compromise.
findings.recommendation: Set enable_key_rotation = true to rotate KMS symmetric keys annually.

findings.name: CloudFront viewer protocol policy allows plain HTTP
findings.description: Allowing HTTP exposes content and cookies to interception during transit.
findings.recommendation: Set viewer_protocol_policy = 'redirect-to-https' or 'https-only'.

findings.name: CloudFront minimum TLS version below TLSv1.2
findings.description: TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are deprecated with known vulnerabilities (BEAST, POODLE). Connections using old TLS versions can be attacked.
findings.recommendation: Set minimum_protocol_version = 'TLSv1.2_2021' or higher.

findings.name: ElastiCache at-rest encryption disabled
findings.description: Unencrypted ElastiCache data is exposed if the underlying storage is compromised.
findings.recommendation: Set at_rest_encryption_enabled = true for all ElastiCache replication groups.

findings.name: ElastiCache in-transit encryption disabled
findings.description: Without in-transit encryption, data between application and cache is sent in plaintext over the network.
findings.recommendation: Set transit_encryption_enabled = true and configure auth_token for Redis clusters.

findings.name: ECS container running in privileged mode
findings.description: A privileged container has root access to the host EC2 instance, enabling full host compromise.
findings.recommendation: Remove privileged = true. Use task IAM roles for specific AWS permissions instead of privileged mode.

findings.name: OpenSearch domain does not enforce HTTPS
findings.description: Allowing HTTP connections to OpenSearch exposes search queries and indexed data to interception.
findings.recommendation: Set enforce_https = true in domain_endpoint_options.

findings.name: Hardcoded password or secret in Terraform resource
findings.description: Hardcoded passwords or secrets in Terraform files are exposed to anyone with access to the code repository.
findings.recommendation: Use AWS Secrets Manager or SSM Parameter Store with data sources to inject secrets at runtime.

findings.name: RDS instance publicly accessible
findings.description: Making the RDS instance publicly accessible exposes the database endpoint to the internet, enabling brute-force and injection attacks.
findings.recommendation: Set publicly_accessible = false. Place RDS instances in private subnets accessible only from application tier.

findings.name: EBS volume not encrypted
findings.description: Unencrypted EBS volumes expose data if the underlying storage is compromised or the snapshot is shared.
findings.recommendation: Set encrypted = true on all EBS volumes. Enable account-level EBS encryption by default in AWS settings.

findings.name: ECR repository uses mutable image tags
findings.description: Mutable image tags allow container images to be silently replaced, enabling supply chain attacks.
findings.recommendation: Set image_tag_mutability = 'IMMUTABLE' to prevent image tag overwriting.

findings.name: AWS Config recorder does not record all resource types
findings.description: Limiting Config to specific resource types creates blind spots in compliance monitoring and change tracking.
findings.recommendation: Set all_supported = true and include_global_resource_types = true inside the recording_group block.

findings.name: AWS Config recorder excludes global resource types (IAM)
findings.description: Excluding global resources (e.g., IAM) from Config leaves critical identity changes untracked.
findings.recommendation: Set include_global_resource_types = true inside the recording_group block.

findings.name: SageMaker notebook instance allows direct internet access
findings.description: Enabling direct internet access for a SageMaker notebook allows data exfiltration and bypasses VPC security controls.
findings.recommendation: Set direct_internet_access = 'Disabled' and route traffic through a VPC with a NAT gateway.
